raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Surveillance Pricing Protection Act (AB 446) will prohibit the practice of surveillance pricing by making it unlawful for businesses to use personal data when charging different prices for the same product or service.",
    "Many companies offer dynamic pricing based on market factors, but surveillance pricing utilizes a customer's individualized data profile to maximize their spending.",
    "Surveillance pricing is described as predatory, discriminatory, and a violation of public trust.",
    "Companies are using personal data against consumers to charge different prices for the same exact product.",
    "Without legal protections and stronger regulations, companies will continue to use opaque pricing practices that maximize profits at the expense of ordinary people.",
    "Digital shelf tags and facial recognition technology can enable prices to change minute to minute."
  ]
}
```
